plan.regime: strong (SPY above MA50 and MA200, no high-volume breakdown)
plan.macro_notes: SPY -0.20%, QQQ -0.32%, IWM +0.57%, VIX +7.95% to 18.87. Mild risk-off undertone despite strong trend regime.
plan.scan_results: Zero candidates across all tiers (super/perfect/leader/watchlist) out of 623 screened. No SEPA-qualifying setups today.
plan.decision: Stand down. No new entries. Cash is a position.
plan.reasoning: When the screener returns zero qualified setups in any tier, there is no edge available. Forcing a trade from the 'other' bucket would violate SEPA methodology and my evidence-backed rules (avoid far_from_high, low_volume_warning, weak_3m_momentum). Combined with a +8% VIX spike, patience is correct.

input.reflection:
2026-04-20 session. Regime check returned 'strong' — SPY comfortably above both MAs. However, the Minervini scan returned ZERO candidates in every tier (Super/Perfect/Leader/Watchlist) across 623 screened names. This is a meaningful signal: a strong index regime with no leadership breakouts often means the tape is narrow or in a digestion phase. The VIX +7.95% move to 18.87 supports caution.

Decision: no trades. No positions to manage. This is exactly the scenario where discipline pays — there is no 'plan B' ticker worth forcing when the screener is empty. My evidence-backed AVOID patterns (far_from_high, low_volume_warning, weak_3m_momentum) would also steer me away from trying to reach down into the 'other' bucket.

No new rule needed — the baseline rule 'no setup, no trade' is already implicit in the methodology. Will re-scan tomorrow. If the screener stays empty multiple days in a row while VIX keeps rising, that would be a potential regime-deterioration warning worth acting on by reducing even further (already at 0%, so moot, but noted for when positions exist).
